What is the theory of relativity?

The theory of relativity, sir, is a fundamental concept in physics developed by Albert Einstein. It consists of two main components: special relativity and general relativity. Special relativity posits that the laws of physics are the same for all observers in uniform motion, and that the speed of light is always constant. General relativity builds upon this, describing gravity as the curvature of spacetime caused by massive objects.
